notes: Single-pass blind run of the haberlah/wardley-mapping skill on the healthcare clinical decision-making scenario. Executed SKILL.md Steps 1-7 verbatim: answered Step 1 strategic-context questions from the scenario itself (no user clarification available); built a 20-component value chain (4 anchors: Patient, Practitioner, Institution, Payer; 16 non-anchor); used haberlah's own evolution ranges (Genesis 0.00-0.17, Custom-Built 0.18-0.39, Product 0.40-0.69, Commodity 0.70-1.00) per reference/evolution-assessment.md; did Step 4 manual validation (positions in [0,1], no cycles, no close pairs <0.06 on both axes, all evolve targets rightward); did NOT run our in-tree validator or iterate against it. Produced all three deliverables per skill contract: React/SVG artifact embedded in output.md, raw OWM block in draft.owm + duplicated in output.md, strategic commentary with per-component rationale table, doctrine check, build-vs-buy table, 12-24 month evolution predictions, and open questions. Divergences from skill workflow: (1) Step 2 recommends 8-15 components; carried 20 because the brief explicitly spans 7 apparatus areas + 4 people-types + the bespoke/industrialised and fairness/exceptions distinctions, and splitting into submaps would fragment the central narrative (the seam between bespoke judgement and industrialised process); noted and accepted the readability cost. (2) Used 4 anchors rather than 1 to preserve the Patient/Practitioner/Institution/Payer tension that the scenario specifically calls out; OWM grammar supports multi-anchor. (3) No existing OWM input, so skipped parse_owm.py branch. No access to precise token/time telemetry in this harness.
